meta_title: Spring Window Cleaning Shuswap | Wagon Windows
meta_desc: Post-winter window cleaning in Salmon Arm & the Shuswap. Clear pollen, road grime & lake runoff before summer. Book your spring clean — slots fill fast.
h1: Spring Window Cleaning in the Shuswap — April & May
urgency_note: Spring slots typically fill by the first week of April — book early to guarantee your preferred date.
intro: Winter in the Shuswap leaves its mark on every window — road salt spray, freeze-thaw grime rings, and a winter's worth of settled dust. Spring cleaning isn't just cosmetic; it protects your glass before the high-UV Okanagan summer hits. A clean done right in April or May means streak-free glass all the way through August.
why_this_season: The Shuswap spring runs a compressed, punishing timeline for windows. By late March, snowmelt and runoff carry road-salt residue, fine silt, and organic matter from surrounding fields and the Salmon River corridor right onto exterior glass — especially on properties near the Trans-Canada or Adams River road. April brings the cottonwood pollen season, which coats surfaces in a sticky film that bonds with morning dew and etches into glass if left through May. Lake-adjacent homes on Shuswap Lake, Mara Lake, and Mabel Lake face an additional challenge: spring wind events kick up mineral-laden lake spray that deposits calcium and silica on exposed glass. Left through summer UV, these deposits begin the etching process that leads to permanent hard water staining. The Shuswap's elevation also means a late, fast spring — freeze-thaw cycles continue well into April, and any water trapped in grime layers on your glass is still expanding and contracting against the surface. Getting a professional clean in April removes the winter layer before UV heat locks it in, and sets your glass up for the longest possible clean window through the summer. For seasonal property owners on Blind Bay, Sorrento, and Scotch Creek, spring is the window between ice-off and your first guests arriving.
what_we_do: Full exterior scrub to remove winter road salt, cottonwood pollen film, and lake-spray mineral deposits
Interior window clean available as an add-on — ideal after a winter of condensation buildup on older frames
Screen inspection and cleaning — screens clog with pollen and debris over winter, reducing airflow and trapping dirt
Track and sill wipe-down to clear the winter's accumulated grit before it grinds against your frames all summer
pricing_note: Residential spring cleans start at $169 for exterior-only on smaller homes. Interior + exterior packages available. Screens $4–$5 each. Contact us for a free quote on larger or lakefront properties.

faqs.q: Why do spring cleans book up so fast in the Shuswap?
faqs.a: The Shuswap spring is short — it runs roughly six weeks between reliable thaw and the summer dry spell. Most homeowners want their windows done before May long weekend, and there are only so many working days in that window. We typically have a waitlist by the second week of April, so booking in March is the safest move if you want a guaranteed spring slot.

faqs.q: Is interior window cleaning worth it in spring specifically?
faqs.a: Yes — winter creates condensation on interior glass, especially in homes with wood heat or older window seals. That condensation leaves mineral haze and sometimes mould specks on interior sills and glass edges. If you haven't had the interiors done in 12+ months, spring is the best time: you're already opening the house up after winter, and the difference is immediately noticeable when the sun finally hits.

faqs.q: Do you clean windows in light rain or overcast conditions?
faqs.a: Yes. Overcast conditions are actually ideal for window cleaning — direct sun causes cleaning solution to dry before it can be properly removed, leaving streaks. Light rain is fine for exterior work. We only reschedule in heavy rain, high wind, or if overnight temperatures are forecast to dip below freezing, which can happen in the Shuswap into late April.

faqs.q: My windows have a yellowish film from cottonwood pollen. Does that come off?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — if it was deposited this season. Cottonwood pollen is sticky but not abrasive, so a thorough professional scrub with the right solution removes it cleanly. If the film has been baked on through one or more summers of UV exposure, it may have begun to bond with the glass surface, at which point we assess it on-site and quote accordingly for stain treatment.

meta_title: Summer Window Cleaning Shuswap | Wagon Windows
meta_desc: Beat the summer haze on Shuswap windows. Dust, UV film & vacation rental turnarounds — Wagon Windows keeps your glass clear all season. Book now.
h1: Summer Window Cleaning in the Shuswap — June through August
urgency_note: Vacation rental turnarounds are time-sensitive — contact us to discuss a recurring schedule that works around your booking calendar.
intro: Shuswap summers are bright, dry, and hard on glass. Road dust from gravel driveways and rural routes coats windows within days, while UV intensity from June through August accelerates the bonding of any existing film. For vacation rental hosts in Blind Bay, Sicamous, and Scotch Creek, clean windows are a direct line to better reviews. For full-time residents, it's simply the season when your view matters most.
why_this_season: The Shuswap sits in one of BC's sunnier interior valleys, and summer UV is intense from June through late August. That UV acts as a curing agent on any film sitting on your glass — dust, pollen residue, insect splatter, and the fine silica particles thrown up by gravel roads all begin to bond more firmly to glass surfaces under sustained heat and light. Properties near the lake pick up boat wake spray and jet-ski mist, which carries dissolved minerals that dry into white haze on exposed lower-storey windows. The summer dry season also means Salmon Arm and the surrounding region get regular dust events from fields east of town and from high-traffic recreation routes like the Squilax-Anglemont Road. For vacation rental owners — and there are thousands of short-term rental properties on Shuswap Lake alone — summer is peak revenue season, and a window clean between guests is the fastest visible upgrade you can make to a property. Dirty windows read immediately in listing photos and during check-in. A mid-summer clean in July splits the season and removes the accumulated film before it bakes through August. For homes with screen porches or large lake-facing windows, a summer clean also clears the insect debris that accumulates from June bug season through the July heat peak.
what_we_do: Exterior scrub targeting road dust, UV-set film, lake mineral haze, and insect debris
Vacation rental turnaround cleans — interior + exterior scheduled around checkout and check-in windows
Screen cleaning to restore airflow and prevent pollen and insect debris from transferring back to glass
Spot treatment assessment for UV-bonded mineral deposits or hard water haze developing on lake-facing windows
pricing_note: Residential summer cleans from $169 exterior-only. Vacation rental and recurring packages available — contact us to discuss a rate that covers your full booking season.

faqs.q: How quickly do windows get dirty again after a summer clean?
faqs.a: In dry conditions with light traffic, a professionally cleaned exterior can stay clear for 6–10 weeks. Properties on gravel roads, near the lake, or with heavy tree coverage will resoil faster — sometimes within 3–4 weeks. Most residential clients find one summer clean sufficient; vacation rental owners often do a mid-season top-up. We're happy to advise based on your property's specific exposure.

faqs.q: Can you work around my vacation rental booking calendar?
faqs.a: Yes — this is something we actively accommodate. If you share your booking calendar, we can identify gap days between checkouts and check-ins and schedule accordingly. For properties with tight turnarounds, we recommend gap days of at least 3–4 hours. We can also set up recurring cleans tied to your season so you're not rebooking each time.

faqs.q: Do you work in the heat of summer? What about smoke season?
faqs.a: We work through summer heat with early-morning scheduling on hot days — direct sun on hot glass can cause streaking, so we prefer morning starts from June through August. During wildfire smoke events, we may reschedule exterior work if visibility or air quality is significantly impaired. We'll always communicate proactively if conditions affect your booking.

faqs.q: My windows are developing a white haze that doesn't wipe off easily. Is that hard water?
faqs.a: Quite possibly. Properties on Shuswap Lake, Mara Lake, and Mabel Lake are especially prone to mineral deposits from lake spray and irrigation water high in calcium and magnesium. Early-stage haze often responds to professional cleaning with appropriate solutions. If the deposit has been baking through multiple summers, it may require targeted stain removal treatment. We assess on-site and quote transparently before any additional treatment.

meta_title: Fall Window Cleaning Shuswap | Wagon Windows
meta_desc: Last chance before the Shuswap freeze. Clear leaf tannins, fall grime & prep gutters before winter. Book your fall clean & gutter bundle in September or October.
h1: Fall Window Cleaning in the Shuswap — September & October
urgency_note: Fall is the last opportunity for a professional exterior clean before freeze-up — once overnight temperatures drop below zero, wet cleaning is no longer possible.
intro: A fall window clean in the Shuswap does double duty: it removes the summer's accumulated grime before it freezes in place, and it clears leaf tannin stains before they etch into the glass over winter. Paired with gutter cleaning — our most popular fall bundle — it puts your whole exterior in good shape heading into November. Once the Shuswap temperatures drop, the window for wet cleaning closes fast.
why_this_season: Fall in the Shuswap brings a specific and underappreciated threat to windows: leaf tannins. As deciduous trees — birch, cottonwood, maple, and the ornamental cherries common in Salmon Arm residential neighbourhoods — drop their leaves, tannin-rich decomposing matter contacts wet glass during morning dew and rain events. Tannins are acidic and leave a brownish, difficult-to-remove film that bonds to glass over the winter months. A fall clean before leaf-fall peaks (typically mid-October in Salmon Arm, slightly later in lower Blind Bay) removes the summer residue and prevents tannins from adhering to dirty glass. Fall is also when the Shuswap's birch-heavy properties shed significant debris into gutters, making the window-and-gutter bundle our most requested fall package. Gutters need to be cleared before the first significant snowfall — clogged gutters in the Shuswap's wet-snow November and December events lead to ice dam formation, which backs water under rooflines. The Shuswap's elevation means hard frost arrives earlier than many homeowners expect: Salmon Arm regularly records first hard frost in early to mid-October, and Sicamous and Chase can see it even earlier. Wet window cleaning below freezing is neither safe nor effective, so the practical fall clean window runs from Labour Day through mid-October.
what_we_do: Full exterior window scrub targeting summer film, early tannin deposits, and post-harvest dust from surrounding agricultural areas
Gutter cleaning — cleared, flushed, and downspouts confirmed flowing before freeze-up
Screen removal and storage prep — screens left through winter are damaged by ice and freeze-thaw cycling
Final interior clean option for homeowners who want a completely fresh start before spending more time indoors over winter
pricing_note: Fall window cleans from $169 exterior-only residential. Gutter cleaning from $189 (assessed on-site). Bundle a window clean with gutter service and ask about combined scheduling on the same visit.

faqs.q: Should I get windows cleaned before or after the leaves fall?
faqs.a: Before is better for the glass, but timing is tight. If you clean before peak leaf-fall (late September for most of Salmon Arm), your glass is clear before tannins start depositing. If you clean during or just after leaf-fall, you catch everything in one pass but should also do gutters. We typically recommend booking mid-October for a combined window and gutter service that closes the season in a single visit.

faqs.q: What's the latest date you can do exterior window cleaning before winter?
faqs.a: We work as long as overnight temperatures stay reliably above zero — typically through mid to late October in Salmon Arm, slightly earlier in higher-elevation communities. We monitor forecasts closely in October and will flag if a booking is cutting close to the freeze window. If a frost event is forecast within 24 hours of your clean, we'll proactively reschedule rather than risk a compromised result.

faqs.q: Why bundle gutter cleaning with window cleaning in fall?
faqs.a: Efficiency, mostly. We're already on-site with ladders and equipment for the windows. Adding gutter cleaning in the same visit saves you a separate booking and typically means faster scheduling. Fall is our highest-demand period for gutter work because Shuswap gutters fill fast with birch, cottonwood, and conifer needles through September and October. Bundling ensures both are done before the first snow event.

faqs.q: Do you remove and store screens as part of a fall service?
faqs.a: We can clean and remove screens during a fall visit — this is a good practice for the Shuswap winter because freeze-thaw cycling stresses screen frames and spline seals. Screens left in through winter often arrive at spring with bent frames and degraded mesh. We remove, clean, and stack screens for you to store in a garage or shed. This is an add-on; mention it when booking.

meta_title: Pre-Season Vacation Home Window Clean | Wagon Windows
meta_desc: Opening your Shuswap vacation property for the season? Wagon Windows handles the pre-season clean in Blind Bay, Sicamous, Sorrento & Scotch Creek. Book March–April.
h1: Pre-Season Vacation Home Window Cleaning — Sorrento, Blind Bay, Sicamous & the Shuswap
urgency_note: Pre-season slots for lakefront communities book out fast as seasonal owners coordinate their opening weekends — contact us in February or early March.
intro: Every spring, thousands of seasonal property owners across the Shuswap return to find their vacation homes carrying a winter's worth of grime, mould, and weather damage on the exterior glass. A professional pre-season clean — done before your first guests arrive or before the long weekend rush — turns a stale winter property into a welcoming summer home in a few hours. We serve the full arc of Shuswap Lake communities, from Sorrento and Blind Bay through Sicamous and Scotch Creek.
why_this_season: Seasonal properties in the Shuswap face a distinct set of challenges compared to year-round homes. Closed up from October through March, vacation homes accumulate condensation on interior glass, mould growth on wooden sills in properties without dehumidifiers, and a full season of exterior weathering without maintenance. The Shuswap's winter includes freeze-thaw cycling through November, December, and March, which drives mineral-laden meltwater across glass surfaces repeatedly. Properties directly on Shuswap Lake, Mara Lake, and the arms of the lake system face winter lake-spray exposure during the November through February wind events that can be significant in exposed bays. Blind Bay properties are particularly exposed to prevailing westerlies that carry mineral-laden spray. Sorrento sits at a slightly lower elevation and warms earlier, making it a prime March clean target. Sicamous properties on the narrows between Shuswap and Mara Lake face spray from both water bodies. Scotch Creek and Lee Creek on the north arm see heavy cottonwood and conifer debris accumulation over winter. The pre-season window — March through mid-April — is narrow. Property owners want the place ready before May long weekend, the traditional Shuswap season opener. We prioritize coordination with property managers and seasonal owners to batch our outlying community visits, keeping costs manageable and turnaround fast.
what_we_do: Full exterior window clean — removing winter mineral deposits, mould spots, and weather film from all accessible glass
Interior window clean — condensation haze, mould specks on sills, and the winter staleness that comes with a closed-up property
Screen inspection and cleaning — screens that sat through winter often need debris clearing before re-installation
Gutter check and clear — winter debris and conifer needles typical of Shuswap lots require a spring flush before the rain season
pricing_note: Pre-season vacation home cleans are priced the same as standard residential service — from $169 exterior-only, from $229 interior + exterior. Travel to Blind Bay, Sorrento, Sicamous, and Scotch Creek is accommodated. Contact us for a quote based on your property.

faqs.q: Can you coordinate directly with my property manager instead of me?
faqs.a: Yes — this is common for seasonal owners who manage their bookings remotely. We can communicate scheduling, access details, and job completion directly with your property manager or caretaker. We send a completion note with details of what was done, and can send photos on request. Just include your property manager's contact information when booking and we'll handle the rest.

faqs.q: Do you serve Blind Bay, Sorrento, Sicamous, and Scotch Creek, or just Salmon Arm?
faqs.a: We serve the full Shuswap — Salmon Arm is our base, but we regularly service Blind Bay, Sorrento, Tappen, Notch Hill, Sicamous, Chase, and Scotch Creek. For outlying communities, we batch multiple jobs on the same route day to keep scheduling efficient. Contact us with your location and we'll confirm availability and any scheduling considerations for your area.

faqs.q: My cabin has been closed since October. What should I expect when you arrive?
faqs.a: For a cabin closed through a full Shuswap winter, expect exterior glass with significant mineral haze from freeze-thaw cycles, possible mould or algae on north-facing windows and sills, and light oxidation on aluminum frames. Interior glass is usually easier — mostly condensation haze — unless there has been a moisture problem. We assess on arrival and complete a standard clean; if we find anything requiring additional treatment, we'll flag it before adding any cost.

faqs.q: What's the best time to book a pre-season clean relative to my opening weekend?
faqs.a: Ideally 1–2 weeks before your first planned use or your first guest arrival. This gives us scheduling flexibility and gives you time to address any issues we flag — like damaged seals or frame problems. Booking in February for an April clean is not too early. Spring pre-season is our second-busiest period, and lakefront community slots in particular go fast as multiple seasonal owners often want service the same week.
